title: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available
display_title: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available
wikitext:
[]: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available?             Quote Post by BritKnee » Sun May 15, 2022 9:56 am Basically are there any 90s fansubs that I can find, it would be even cooler if they were before the AVD Dub. Also this might be a given but if they arent the directors cut then that would be even better.

[]: Re: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available?             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sun May 15, 2022 11:28 am I believe there is a fansub from around that time but I think the ADV dub came first. From my understanding of said fansub, it's pretty spotty in terms of accuracy.

[]: Re: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available?             Quote Post by BritKnee » Sun May 15, 2022 11:34 am hui43210 wrote: pretty spotty in terms of accuracy.

[]: Re: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available?             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sun May 15, 2022 11:39 am BritKnee wrote: hui43210#935579 wrote: pretty spotty in terms of accuracy.

[]: Re: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available?             Quote Post by BritKnee » Sun May 15, 2022 1:14 pm hui43210 wrote: BritKnee#935580 wrote: What is the best way to watch the original broadcasts? I dont have the money for the dvd's so is it on archive or a website like that?

[]: Re: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available?             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon May 30, 2022 1:08 am BritKnee wrote: Basically are there any 90s fansubs that I can find, it would be even cooler if they were before the AVD Dub. Also this might be a given but if they arent the directors cut then that would be even better.

[]: Re: Is there a fansub that came out in the west before any dubs were available?             Quote Post by HRSM » Wed Jun 01, 2022 2:58 am Much of the early versions of the Literal Translation Project definitely predate the first ADV VHS Tapes coming out in August 1996. (There's also a suggestion the translation was done very quickly after the initial TV Tokyo broadcast via some of the included comments in each file, but I can't 100% verify this). The fansub release by E. Monsoon Studios might also predate some of the ADV stuff, but I'm not certain on this front. The 2015 "Archives of Evangelion" release contains the Japanese broadcast masters as they were shown on TV Tokyo and connected networks between Oct 1995 and Mar/Apr 1996 and contain the OA versions of Episodes 21-24. There is also a fansub by "Project X" of the non-director's cut episodes from around that period floating around the internet (It's against site policy to share pirated material, but it's not entirely difficult to find)
summary:

Also this might be a given but if they arent the directors cut then that would be even better.
